item.title: 使用 VoxKraken 彻底改变您的工作流程：使用 GPT-3 说话和聊天的 Google 扩展 - 第 1 部分 | Revolutionize Your Workflow with VoxKraken: A Google Extension for Speaking and Chatting with GPT-3 - Part 1
item.description:
注意：只有当您暂停 Adblock 时，此扩展程序才能阅读文本

虽然它确实可以节省时间，但如果您可以拥有像托尼·斯塔克的贾维斯这样的 AI 合作伙伴呢？ 我知道我有，而且我确信我并不孤单。 我梦想在我的第二个屏幕上有一个聪明的男人或女人，他可以回答我所有的问题，就像我小时候想大声问出所有问题一样。 这就是此解决方案的想法来源。

培养 AI 助手的个性：打造独特且引人入胜的用户体验的第一步

它是在工作时出现的。”——亨利·马蒂斯

产生新想法总是一个挑战，但幸运的是，我们现在有一些很棒的工具可以帮助我们。 这就是为什么我求助于Chat GPT来帮助我为我的助手找到完美的名字。 在收到一份名单后，一个非常合适的名字脱颖而出： “VoxKraken” 。 这个名字听起来既令人印象深刻又令人难忘，这正是我一直在寻找的。 我很高兴继续我的旅程，并通过使用Midjourney沿途生成其他关键视觉效果，如徽标和图像。

设计您的 AI 助手：打造独特而难忘的用户体验的第二步”

为了创造无缝的用户体验，我决定将我的 AI 助手直接添加到 Google 页面上。 虽然您可以在任何地方添加您的助手，但我相信 Google 提供了完美的平台。 借助 OpenAI 和 Chat GPT 等创新工具，我们可以通过多种方式创建有效且引人入胜的界面。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何设计一个界面，以最大限度地发挥这些工具的优势并为用户提供无缝体验。

这是一个简单的过程，包括添加一些有趣的生成标题图片（如果需要）和两列用于显示问题和答案文本。 在此示例中，您可以逐步完成实现此功能所需的步骤并自定义界面以为您的用户提供独特且引人入胜的体验。

听力助教：为 Kraken 开发语音识别的第一步

这是尊重的表现。 这让人们感到被重视。” 对于下一步，我们将使用名为 SpeechRecognition 的库实现SpeechRecognition函数。 这个库既免费又易于设置。 我们将从创建一个可以捕获我们的语音的简单侦听器开始。 一旦听众捕捉到文本，我们就会让我们的 Kraken 大声朗读它，并等待朗读完成。

释放力量：第三步——教我们的 Kraken 自信地说话

这部分也比较简单。 我们将利用 SpeechSynthesis 库让我们的 Kraken 说话。 这是算法：

  • 我们定义新的合成器并收集所有可用的声音。

  • 接下来，我们设置所需的语音并进行相应配置。

  • 最后，我们将在一个生成问题的函数中调用它，并让我们的 Kraken 大声朗读它。

解锁智能：最后一步——教我们的 Kraken 像聪明人一样思考

在我们流程的最后一步，我们将使用 OpenAI 模型“text-davinci-003”，它是 Chat GPT 的核心。 该模型是一种最先进的语言处理工具，已经过大量文本数据的训练，使其能够对范围广泛的查询生成类似人类的响应。 通过利用这种 AI 技术的力量，我们可以教会 Kraken 像人一样思考和反应。

要连接到 OpenAI 并访问“text-davinci-003”模型，我们需要设置一些配置。 这些配置将包括访问 API 所需的身份验证凭据，以及与我们的特定用例相关的任何特定设置。

一旦我们建立了连接并配置了设置，我们就可以向模型提出问题并接收响应。 总的来说，通过利用 AI 和 OpenAI“text-davinci-003”模型的力量，我们可以让 Kraken 能够以一种非常模仿人类思维过程的方式处理和响应信息。

认识 VoxKraken：您的智能新朋友，可以通过语音技术聆听和响应

虽然我们的新助手还不完美，但这是一个开始。 有时它很难理解我的发音，较长的问题可能是一个挑战，但在使用简单、免费的工具构建它时这是可以预料的。 重要的是我已经证明了这个概念——可以在一个晚上构建这样的技术。 现在，我可以快速生成想法列表或学习如何使用新的节点包，从高级开发人员的每日脚本中获得灵感，甚至让它写一条来自我的经理的具有相关上下文的消息。 它更快，这意味着我可以继续编写代码而不会分心。



原文:

ATTENTION: This extension can read the text, only when you pause Adblock

GPT-3 is an amazing tool, but sometimes it can be distracting to use when you need to find information quickly. While it certainly saves time, what if you could have an AI partner like Tony Stark's JARVIS? I know I do, and I'm sure I'm not alone. I dream of having a wise man or woman on my second screen who can answer all of my questions, just like when I was a kid and wanted to ask everything out loud. That's where the idea for this solution came from.

Developing Your AI Assistant's Personality: The First Step to Creating a Unique and Engaging User Experience

“Don’t wait for inspiration. It comes while working." - Henri Matisse

Generating new ideas is always a challenge, but fortunately, we now have some amazing tools to help us. That's why I turned to Chat GPT to assist me in finding the perfect name for my assistant. After receiving a list of names, one stood out as a perfect fit: "VoxKraken". This name sounds both impressive and memorable, which is exactly what I was looking for. I'm excited to continue my journey and generate other key visuals like logos and images along the way by using Midjourney.

Designing Your AI Assistant: The Second Step to Creating a Unique and Memorable User Experience"

"I really believe that everyone has a talent, ability, or skill that he can mine to support himself and to succeed in life." - Dean Koontz

In my quest to create a seamless user experience, I've decided to add my AI assistant directly onto the Google page. While you can add your assistant anywhere you'd like, I believe that Google provides the perfect platform. With innovative tools like OpenAI and Chat GPT at our disposal, there are many ways to create an effective and engaging interface. In this article, we'll explore how to design an interface that maximizes the benefits of these tools and provides a seamless experience for users.

The drawKrakenAssistant function is responsible for adding the Kraken assistant to your Google web page. It's a simple process that involves adding some interesting generated header pictures (if desired) and two columns for displaying the questions and answers text. In this example, you can walk through the steps required to implement this function and customize the interface to provide a unique and engaging experience for your users.

Teaching Assistant to Listen: The First Step in Developing Speech Recognition for Kraken

“To say that a person feels listened to means a lot more than just their ideas get heard. It's a sign of respect. It makes people feel valued.” - Deborah Tannen, Georgetown University

For the next step, we will be implementing a SpeechRecognition function using the library called SpeechRecognition. This library is both free and easy to set up. We will begin by creating a simple listener that can capture our speech. Once the listener captures the text, we will ask our Kraken to read it out loud and wait until the reading is finished.

Unleash the Power: The Third Step - Teaching Our Kraken to Speak with Confidence

This part is also relatively straightforward. We will be utilizing the SpeechSynthesis library for our Kraken to speak. Here's the algorithm:

  • We define our new synthesis and gather all available voices.

  • Next, we set up the desired voice and configure it accordingly.

  • Finally, we will call this in a function that generates a question and have our Kraken read it aloud.

Unlocking Intelligence: The Last Step - Teaching Our Kraken to Think Like a Smart Person

In the final step of our process, we will be utilizing the OpenAI model 'text-davinci-003', which is the core of Chat GPT. This model is a state-of-the-art language processing tool that has been trained on massive amounts of text data, enabling it to generate human-like responses to a wide range of queries. By leveraging the power of this AI technology, we can teach our Kraken to think and respond like a human being.

To connect to OpenAI and access the 'text-davinci-003' model, we will need to set some configurations. These configurations will include things like the authentication credentials required to access the API, as well as any specific settings related to our particular use case.

Once we have established a connection and configured the settings, we can then pose a question to the model and receive a response. The 'text-davinci-003' model is incredibly versatile and can answer questions on a wide range of topics, making it an excellent tool for teaching our Kraken to think critically and respond intelligently to a variety of situations.

Overall, by leveraging the power of AI and the OpenAI 'text-davinci-003' model, we can empower our Kraken with the ability to process and respond to information in a way that closely mimics human thought processes.

Meet VoxKraken: Your New Smart Friend Who Can Listen and Respond with Voice Technology

Although our new assistant isn't perfect yet, it's a start. Sometimes it struggles with understanding my pronunciation, and longer questions can be a challenge, but that's to be expected when using simple, free tools to build it. The important thing is that I've proven the concept - that it's possible to build such technology in just one evening. Now, I can quickly generate a list of ideas or learn how to use a new node package, get inspired by a daily script for senior developers, or even have it write a message from my manager with relevant context. It's faster, which means I can continue writing code without getting distracted.

item.title: 征服 Bazel 的学习曲线：我持续的奋斗和充满希望的地平线📈🌅 | Conquering the Learning Curve of Bazel: My Ongoing Struggles and Hopeful Horizon 📈🌅
item.description:
亚绍丹·辛格·拉托尔

发表于 2 月 20 日

嘿大家！ 这个惊人的构建工具有很大的潜力，但对我来说，有一点学习曲线😩📈。

我面临的一个特殊挑战是 rules_nodejs 🚫。 作为一名开发人员，我的首选工具之一是 Storybook JS 📚🤓，但似乎几乎不可能使用 rules_nodejs 😵 运行它。 我花了无数个小时试图让它工作，但它没有发生。

但是，希望就在眼前！ 我一定会很快分享我使用 rules_js 的经验。

总的来说，我认为重要的是要记住学习新技术需要时间和耐心。 一旦暂停，dsfx3d 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，dsfx3d 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dsfx3d 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dsfx3d 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 dsfx3d 的方法：

降低 dsfx3d 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 dsfx3d 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Yashodhan Singh Rathore

Posted on Feb 20

Hey everyone! 🤗 Today, I wanted to talk about something that's been on my mind lately: 🤔 Bazel! This amazing build tool has SO much potential, but for me, there's been a bit of a learning curve 😩📈.

One particular challenge I've faced is with rules_nodejs 🚫. As a developer, one of my go-to tools is Storybook JS 📚🤓, but it seems almost IMPOSSIBLE to run it with rules_nodejs 😵. I've spent COUNTLESS hours trying to get it to work, but it's just not happening.

BUT, there's hope on the horizon! 🌅 I've been hearing great things about rules_js, and I'm optimistic that it will fix all the problems I'm facing with rules_nodejs. 🙏🤞 Fingers crossed! I'll be sure to share my experiences with rules_js soon.

Overall, I think it's important to remember that learning a new technology takes time and patience. 🕰️🧘 Even though I'm struggling with Bazel right now, I know that with persistence and a bit of help from the community, I'll get there eventually. 🤝

>> Check out this classic DEV post <<

Heemali Chaudhari - Feb 20

item.title: 使用 AWS Lambda 的 InstaStalker（还有 SNS） | InstaStalker using AWS Lambda (SNS too)
item.description:
首先不要问我为什么😂


项目

主要目标是抓取 Instagram 个人资料并了解个人资料是公开的还是私人的。 另一方面，这是一个跟踪应用程序，我们假设我们要跟踪的个人资料是私人的。 因此，如果配置文件可见性状态更改为可见（公开），Lambda 会触发 SNS 发送电子邮件。 整个场景无需登录即可运行（至少无需我的凭据）。

刮痧

抓取完全是一个反复试验的阶段。 首先我开始使用 beautifulsoup4 来获取页面。 一开始，它奏效了。 我得到了整个页面源代码 (HTML) 并使用正则表达式解析了“is_private”键。 瞧！ 但是，它的工作时间很短。 所以这就是它在短时间内起作用的原因。 此外，Instagram 经常更改许多内容。 当您达到限制时，bs4 获取登录页面的 HTML，这意味着没有is_private键。

正如我提到的，由于 IP 限制和频繁更改，我需要找到一种新方法。 我在谷歌上搜索并找到了一种从 Instagram API 获取数据的方法。 它比解析 HTML 好得多。

尽管如此，我还是找不到登录的解决方案。 虽然 API 在几次请求后已经检索到用户信息，但仍然需要登录。我尝试了一些 try & except 块并添加了标头（用户代理、编码、模式等），但它们从未奏效。 另外，我尝试了instaloader但得到了同样的错误； 登录。我看到了一些代理解决方案，但这些是付费服务，我不会为它们付费。 然后我发现了RapidAPI 。 它是一个API平台，提供Proxy。 最后，我找到了我要找的东西。 我尝试了一些并决定继续使用其中一个。

代码 V3（目前最新的 😀）

 def rapid_checker(user_name: str): url = "http://api_url/{0}".format(user_name) headers = { "X-RapidAPI-Key": "api_key", "X-RapidAPI-Host": "api_host", } response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers) if response.status_code == 200: response = json.loads(response.text) return { "username": response["username"], "fullname": response["full_name"], "bio": response["biography"], "is_private": response["is_private"], "follower_count": int(response["edge_followed_by"]["count"]), "following_count": int(response["edge_follow"]["count"]), "total_post": int(response["edge_owner_to_timeline_media"]["count"]), "profile_pic": response["profile_pic_url_hd"], } else: print("Couldn't retrieve data for {0}. Reason: {1}".format(user_name, json.loads(response.text)["message"])) return None

最后，我可以在没有任何登录错误的情况下收集数据。 不过API是有每日限额的，不过是可以忍受的事情。

然后我使用 Lambda 通过了自动化阶段。 我使用收集功能创建了一个lambda_handler.py并为通知添加了 SN​​S 代码。 整个剧本👇🏻

 import boto3 import os import requests import json from json import JSONDecodeError client = boto3.client("sns", region_name="region") def rapid_checker(user_name: str): # same as the code above 👆🏻. def lambda_handler(event, context): try: topic_arn = "sns_arn" try: username = event["user_name"] except: username = "amazonwebservices" print("Username:", username) result = rapid_checker(username) if result is not None: print(result) if result["is_private"] != True: client.publish( TopicArn=topic_arn, Subject="🚨🚨🚨", Message="⚠ '{0}' profile is now public! \n \ Follower Count: {1} \n \ Following Count: {2} \n \ Total Post: {3} \n \ Profile Pic: {4}".format( result["username"], result["follower_count"], result["following_count"], result["total_post"], result["profile_pic"], subtype="html"),) print("Message sent to SNS!") print("Checked for:", username, "and the visibility status is:", result["is_private"]) return result else: print("Couldn't retrieve data for {0}. Reason: API Limit Reached!".format(username)) except JSONDecodeError as error: print(error) return "Couldn't retrieve data for {0}".format(username)

接下来我创建了一个 Dockerfile 并将其推送到 ECR。 差不多好了！

 FROM public.ecr.aws/lambda/python:3.8 RUN pip install requests COPY lambda_function.py . CMD [ "lambda_function.lambda_handler" ]

然后我使用 Docker 映像创建了一个 Lambda 函数并对其进行了测试。 一切都很完美。 最后，我为计划的运行创建了一个 EventBridge 规则。

截图👇🏻

这就是您如何使用 AWS 构建您的“自制”Instagram Stalking 应用程序😀

最后别问我为什么😂
由于 Libuv，Node.js 不会停止运行其他操作，Libuv 是一个 C++ 库，负责事件循环和异步处理网络请求、DNS 解析、文件系统操作、数据加密等任务。

当 Node.js 处理数据库查询等任务时，幕后会发生什么？ 我们将按照这段代码逐步探索它。

一旦被暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

取消暂停后，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 aws-builders：

降低 aws-builders 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

First of all don't ask me why 😅


Image Source

Project

InstaStalker = Python + Docker + Lambda + SNS (and touch of EventBridge)
InstaStalker: Your daily stalking assistant.

The main goal is to scrape Instagram profiles and know if a profile is public or private. On the other hand, this is a tracking application, and we assume that the profile we want to follow is private. Therefore, Lambda triggers SNS to send an email if the profile visibility status has changed to visible (public). The whole scenario works without login (at least without my credentials).

Scraping

Scraping was completely a trial and error stage. First I started using beautifulsoup4 to get the page. In the beginning, it worked. I got the whole page source (HTML) and parsed the “is_private” key using regex. Voila! However, it worked for a short time. Instagram allows you to see a few pages without login per IP. So this is why it worked for a short time. Also, Instagram changes many things frequently. When you reach the limit bs4 gets the login page’s HTML, meaning there is no is_private key.

V1 Code

from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from urllib.request import urlopen, Request username = "amazonwebservices"
req = Request("https://instagram.com/" + str(user_name), headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"})
html = urlopen(req).read().decode("utf-8")
soup = BeautifulSoup(html, features="lxml")
scripts = soup.find_all("script") # script block contains user info
...
# then regex part and convert key-values into pandas df

As I mentioned, I needed to find a new way due to IP restriction and frequent changes. I was Googling and found a way to get data from Instagram API. It was much better than parsing HTML.

V2 Code

username = "amazonwebservices"
import json
import requests response = requests.get("https://i.instagram.com/api/v1/users/web_profile_info/?username={0}".format(username), headers={"x-ig-app-id": "936619743392459"})
data = json.loads(response.content)
print(data['data']['user']['is_private'])
# False

Still, I couldn't find a solution for login. Although the API had retrieved the user information after a few requests, it was still necessary to log in. I tried some try & except blocks and added headers (user agents, encoding, schema, etc.), but they never worked out. Also, I tried instaloader but got the same error; log in. I saw some Proxy solutions, but these were paid services, and I wouldn't pay for them. Then I discovered RapidAPI. It is an API platform and provides Proxy. Finally, I found what I was looking for. I tried a few and decided to move forward with one of them.

Code V3 (latest for now 😅)

def rapid_checker(user_name: str): url = "http://api_url/{0}".format(user_name) headers = { "X-RapidAPI-Key": "api_key", "X-RapidAPI-Host": "api_host", } response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers) if response.status_code == 200: response = json.loads(response.text) return { "username": response["username"], "fullname": response["full_name"], "bio": response["biography"], "is_private": response["is_private"], "follower_count": int(response["edge_followed_by"]["count"]), "following_count": int(response["edge_follow"]["count"]), "total_post": int(response["edge_owner_to_timeline_media"]["count"]), "profile_pic": response["profile_pic_url_hd"], } else: print("Couldn't retrieve data for {0}. Reason: {1}".format(user_name, json.loads(response.text)["message"])) return None

Lambda Deployment

Finally, I can gather data without any login errors. However, there is a daily limit for the APIs, but it is a tolerable thing.

Then I passed through the automatization stage using Lambda. I create a lambda_handler.py using the gathering function and add SNS codes for the notifications. Whole script 👇🏻

import boto3
import os
import requests
import json
from json import JSONDecodeError client = boto3.client("sns", region_name="region") def rapid_checker(user_name: str): # same as the code above 👆🏻.

def lambda_handler(event, context): try: topic_arn = "sns_arn" try: username = event["user_name"] except: username = "amazonwebservices" print("Username:", username) result = rapid_checker(username) if result is not None: print(result) if result["is_private"] != True: client.publish( TopicArn=topic_arn, Subject="🚨🚨🚨", Message="⚠ '{0}' profile is now public! \n \
 Follower Count: {1} \n \
 Following Count: {2} \n \
 Total Post: {3} \n \
 Profile Pic: {4}".format( result["username"], result["follower_count"], result["following_count"], result["total_post"], result["profile_pic"], subtype="html"),) print("Message sent to SNS!") print("Checked for:", username, "and the visibility status is:", result["is_private"]) return result else: print("Couldn't retrieve data for {0}. Reason: API Limit Reached!".format(username)) except JSONDecodeError as error: print(error) return "Couldn't retrieve data for {0}".format(username)

Next I created a Dockerfile and pushed it to ECR. Almost ready!


FROM public.ecr.aws/lambda/python:3.8
RUN pip install requests
COPY lambda_function.py . CMD [ "lambda_function.lambda_handler" ]

Then I created a Lambda function using a Docker image and tested it. Everything works perfectly. Finally, I created an EventBridge Rule for the scheduled runs.

Screenshots 👇🏻

This is how you can build your "homemade" Instagram Stalking application using AWS 😅

Lastly don't ask me why 😅
(All solutions arise from a need...)

Node.js doesn’t stop from running other operations because of Libuv, a C++ library responsible for the event loop and asynchronously handling tasks such as network requests, DNS resolution, file system operations, data encryption, etc.

What happens under the hood when Node.js works on tasks such as database queries? We will explore it by following this piece of code step by step.

Ajeet Singh Raina - Jan 21

Once suspended, aws-builders will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aws-builders will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aws-builders will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aws-builders is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag aws-builders:

Make all posts by aws-builders less visible

aws-builders consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging aws-builders will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: silverstone

item.title: Python 中的可靠原则 | Solid Principles in Python
item.description:
• 单一职责原则 (SRP)
  • 开闭原则（OCP）
  • 里氏替换原则 (LSP)
  • 接口隔离原则 (ISP)
  • 依赖倒置原则（DIP）
  • 这一原则规定类应该只有一个责任。
  • 违反SRP的代码是：
 class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def greet(self): print(f"Hello, my name is {self.name} and I'm {self.age} years old.") def celebrate_birthday(self): self.age += 1 print(f"{self.name} is now {self.age} years old!")
  • 在上面的代码中，Person 类有两个职责，一个是问候，另一个是庆祝生日，按照SRP创建一个不同的类，一个处理问候，另一个处理生日庆祝。
 class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def greet(self): print(f"Hello, my name is {self.name} and I'm {self.age} years old.") class BirthdayCelebrator: def celebrate_birthday(self, person): person.age += 1 print(f"{person.name} is now {person.age} years old!")
  • 该原则指出类应该对修改关闭但对扩展开放。
  • 违反OCP的代码是：
 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = height def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): if isinstance(shape, Rectangle): return shape.area() elif isinstance(shape, Circle): return shape.area() elif isinstance(shape, Triangle): return shape.area()
  • 在上面的代码中，如果我们尝试创建一个新类，例如六边形，我们需要修改 AreaCalculator 类，这将违反OCP 。
  • 要更正代码，我们只需要更改 AreaCalculator 类的方法，因此无需修改 AreaCalculator 类。
 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()
  • 该原则指出使用子类创建的对象可以替代使用父类创建的对象。
  • 违反LSP的代码是：
 class Bird: def fly(self): print("The bird is flying") class Penguin(Bird): def fly(self): raise Exception("I can't fly") def make_bird_fly(bird): bird.fly() bird = Bird() make_bird_fly(bird) # Output: The bird is flying penguin = Penguin() make_bird_fly(penguin) # Output: An exception is raised
  • 在上面的代码中，企鹅会在我们调用 fly 方法时抛出错误。 要更正它，只需执行方法 ovveridding。
 class Bird: def fly(self): print("The bird is flying") class Penguin(Bird): def fly(self): print("I can't fly") def make_bird_fly(bird): bird.fly() bird = Bird() make_bird_fly(bird) # Output: The bird is flying penguin = Penguin() make_bird_fly(penguin) # output: I can't fly
  • 这一原则指出，最好有许多小的、特定的接口，而不是一个大的、通用的接口。
  • 违反ISP的代码是：
 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Animal(ABC): @abstractmethod def eat(self): pass @abstractmethod def sleep(self): pass @abstractmethod def move(self): pass class Dog(Animal): def eat(self): print("The dog is eating") def sleep(self): print("The dog is sleeping") def move(self): print("The dog is running")
  • 上面的代码违反了ISP ，就好像我们试图使用动物类创建鱼类，但它没有移动能力，所以我们需要创建所有动物共有的方法，如进食、睡眠和繁殖。
  • 遵守ISP 的代码是：
 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Animal(ABC): @abstractmethod def eat(self): pass @abstractmethod def sleep(self): pass @abstractmethod def reproduce(self): pass
  • 上面的方法在所有动物中都很常见，因此它是动物最简单的接口，并且将根据同类中的其他动物添加另一种方法。
  • 该原则指出实体必须依赖于抽象，而不是具体化。 它声明高层模块不能依赖于低层模块，但它们应该依赖于抽象。
  • 违反DIP的代码是：
 class Shape: def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()
  • 遵循DIP 的代码是：
 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()

永恒的开发帖子...

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

开发人员使用最多的技术不是 Javascript。

它不是 Python 或 HTML。

它甚至很少在面试中被提及或被列为工作的先决条件。

当然，我说的是 Git 和版本控制。

一旦暂停，nk1fc 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，nk1fc 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，nk1fc 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 nk1fc 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 nk1fc 的方法：

降低 nk1fc 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 nk1fc 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

  • The Single-Responsibility Principle (SRP)
  • The Open-Closed Principle (OCP)
  • The Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP)
  • The Interface Segregation Principle (ISP)
  • The Dependency inversion Principle (DIP)
  • This principle state that the Class should have only one responsibility.
  • Code That violates SRP is: 
class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def greet(self): print(f"Hello, my name is {self.name} and I'm {self.age} years old.") def celebrate_birthday(self): self.age += 1 print(f"{self.name} is now {self.age} years old!")
  • In the above code, the Person class have two responsibility one is to greet and another one is to celebrate a birthday to follow the SRP create a different class one which handles greeting and the other handles birthday celebration. 
class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def greet(self): print(f"Hello, my name is {self.name} and I'm {self.age} years old.") class BirthdayCelebrator: def celebrate_birthday(self, person): person.age += 1 print(f"{person.name} is now {person.age} years old!")
  • This principle states that the class should be closed for modification but open for extension.
  • Code that violates OCP is: 
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = height def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): if isinstance(shape, Rectangle): return shape.area() elif isinstance(shape, Circle): return shape.area() elif isinstance(shape, Triangle): return shape.area()
  • In the above code if we try to create a new class such as a hexagon we need to modify the AreaCalculator class which will violate OCP.
  • To correct the code we just need to change the method of AreaCalculator class so there will be no need to modify AreaCalculator class. 
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()
  • This principle states that the object created using the child class can replace the object created using The parent class.
  • Code that violates LSP is: 
class Bird: def fly(self): print("The bird is flying") class Penguin(Bird): def fly(self): raise Exception("I can't fly") def make_bird_fly(bird): bird.fly() bird = Bird()
make_bird_fly(bird) # Output: The bird is flying

penguin = Penguin()
make_bird_fly(penguin) # Output: An exception is raised
  • In the above code, the penguin will raise the error when we call the fly method. To correct it simply do method ovveridding. 
class Bird: def fly(self): print("The bird is flying") class Penguin(Bird): def fly(self): print("I can't fly") def make_bird_fly(bird): bird.fly() bird = Bird()
make_bird_fly(bird) # Output: The bird is flying

penguin = Penguin()
make_bird_fly(penguin) # output: I can't fly
  • This principle states that it is better to have many small, specific interfaces, rather than one large, general-purpose interface.
  • Code that violates ISP is: 
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Animal(ABC): @abstractmethod def eat(self): pass @abstractmethod def sleep(self): pass @abstractmethod def move(self): pass class Dog(Animal): def eat(self): print("The dog is eating") def sleep(self): print("The dog is sleeping") def move(self): print("The dog is running")
  • Above code violates the ISP as if we try to create a fish class using an animal class but it doesn't have ability to move so we need to create methods which are common in all animals such as eating, sleep, and reproduction.
  • Code that adheres ISP is: 
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Animal(ABC): @abstractmethod def eat(self): pass @abstractmethod def sleep(self): pass @abstractmethod def reproduce(self): pass
  • above method is common in all animals hence it is the simplest interface for animals and another method will be added according to other animals in their class.
  • This principle state that Entities must depend on abstractions, not on concretions. It states that the high-level module must not depend on the low-level module, but they should depend on abstractions.
  • Code that violates DIP is: 
class Shape: def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()
  • Code that adheres DIP is: 
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Shape(ABC): @abstractmethod def area(self): pass class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, width, height): self.width = width self.height = height def area(self): return self.width * self.height class Circle(Shape): def __init__(self, radius): self.radius = radius def area(self): return 3.14 * self.radius ** 2 class Triangle(Shape): def __init__(self, base, height): self.base = base self.height = heigh.t def area(self): return 0.5 * self.base * self.height class AreaCalculator: def calculate_area(self, shape): return shape.area()

Timeless DEV post...

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

Vincent A. Cicirello - Jan 11

Elijah Trillionz - Feb 13

Once suspended, nk1fc will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, nk1fc will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by nk1fc will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If nk1fc is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag nk1fc:

Make all posts by nk1fc less visible

nk1fc consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging nk1fc will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: nk1fc

item.title: 扎实的概念 | SOLID concepts
item.description:
一、接口隔离原则

  • 为什么选择 SOLID 和其他软件设计原则？

    当软件到达其第一个版本时，它是干净而优雅的。 当需求发生任何变化并且该变化导致软件不干净和优雅时，就会出现问题，然后在发布几个版本后导致重新设计。 他们是：

    • 刚性 - 软件变得太难实施更改。 每个更改都会导致级联更改。
    • 脆弱性——当软件的一部分发生变化时，另一部分就会崩溃，这与软件无关。
    • 不动性——当软件的一部分难以重用时导致再次重写它。
    • 粘性（设计）——当存在多种方式来实施变更时，保留变更的设计是难以实施的。
    • 粘性（环境）——变革的实施缓慢且效率低下。

    单一职责原则3

    单一职责原则指出每个软件模块都应该有一个且只有一个更改原因。 来自其他人/部门/团队的变更请求不应导致软件相同部分的变更。

    它本质上意味着软件应该根据请求的利益相关者分成不同的类或模块，因此意味着类或模块具有单一的责任。

    例子：

     class Employee(): def calculate_pay(): pass def save(): pass def report_hours(): pass

    对于此示例，请考虑一个在高层设有 CFO、CTO 和 COO 的组织。 这里calculate_pay方法分配给CFO，report_hours方法分配给COO，保存所有数据的save方法将分配给Employee类中的CTO。 每当任何C级人员发生任何变化时，都不会影响分配给其他C级人员的员工的其他部分。

    开闭原则

    开闭原则指出模块应该对扩展开放，对修改关闭。 这意味着当我们要更改模块时，我们应该能够在不更改模块代码的情况下更改它们。 这是通过使用抽象和多态性来实现的。

    例子：

     class Modem(ABC): @abstractmethod def dial(self, phoneNumber): pass class AnalogModem(Modem): def dial(self, phoneNumber): print(f"Dialing {phoneNumber} using an analog modem.") class DigitalModem(Modem): def dial(self, phoneNumber): print(f"Dialing {phoneNumber} using a digital modem.") def logOn(modem: Modem, phoneNumber, username, password): modem.dial(phoneNumber) analog_modem = AnalogModem() digital_modem = DigitalModem() logOn(analog_modem, "12345", "user", "pass") logOn(digital_modem, "67890", "user", "pass")

    在上面的示例中，logOn 函数通过实施模拟和数字调制解调器打开以进行修改，但是从 logOn 函数关闭。 将来创建新型调制解调器时，无需修改登录代码，但可以更改登录。

    里氏替换原则

    使用继承时，派生类必须能够替代基类的使用。 如果基类和派生类之间有任何重大变化，那么就不应该有继承。

    例子：

     class Animal: def make_sound(self): pass class Dog(Animal): def make_sound(self): print("bark") class Cat(Animal): def make_sound(self): print("meow") def make_animal_sound(animal: Animal): animal.make_sound() # Usage: dog = Dog() cat = Cat() make_animal_sound(dog) # bark make_animal_sound(cat) # meow

    在上面的示例中，我们可以将带有 Animal 基类的 cat 和 dog 实例作为参数传递给函数，它仍然可以按预期工作。

    接口隔离原则

    接口隔离原则指出，许多客户端接口优于一个通用接口。 这意味着如果一个类有多个客户，而不是用客户需要的所有方法加载类，为每个客户提供特定的接口并将它们相乘地继承到类中。 这将帮助客户继承一个他们不需要知道他们不会使用的接口部分的类。

    例子：

     class Printer(ABC): @abstractmethod def print(self, document): pass class Fax(ABC): @abstractmethod def fax(self, document): pass class Scanner(ABC): @abstractmethod def scan(self, document): pass class MultiFunctionDevice(Printer, Fax, Scanner): def print(self, document): print(f"Printer") def fax(self, document): print(f"Fax") def scan(self, document): print(f"Scanner") class SimplePrinter(Printer): def print(self, document): print(f"modern printer")

    在这里，打印机、传真和扫描仪接口全部或单独实现，而不是一个庞大的 PrintFaxScan 类，即使它们在现实世界中密切相关。 只实现一个接口的人不知道其他接口。

    依赖倒置原则

    接口隔离原则指出“依赖于抽象。不依赖于具体化。”。 当一个类依赖于其他类时，可能会导致代码重复。 设计中的每个依赖项都应该针对接口或抽象类。 任何依赖项都不应针对具体类。 这是为了确保代码一部分的更改不会导致另一部分代码中断。

    例子：

     class Driver: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def drive(self, vehicle): vehicle.drive() class Vehicle: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def drive(self): pass class Car(Vehicle): def drive(self): print(f"Driving {self.name}") class Boat(Vehicle): def drive(self): print(f"Sailing {self.name}")

    在上面的示例中，如果通过扩展 Car 和 Boat 创建 Driver，则会导致代码重复，并且需要级联更改才能实现对 Driver 的更改。 让 Driver 以类 Vehicle 为目标将有助于它自动使用 Car 和 Boat 类。



    • 原文:

    The SOLID principles1 are principles that help in designing the structure of a program that uses object-oriented programming. SOLID principles are a subset of principles of Object-oriented design by Robert C. Martin2. SOLID is an acronym for the following

    • S - Single Responsibility Principle
    • O - Open-Closed Principle
    • L - Liskov Substitution Principle
    • I - Interface Segregation Principle
    • D - Dependency Inversion Principle

    Why SOLID and other software design principles?

    When software reaches its first release it is clean and elegant. The issue arises when there is any change in requirements and that change causes the software to not be clean and elegant which then results in redesign after a few releases down the road. Robert C. Martin calls this software rot and there are 4 symptoms of rotting. They are:

    • Rigidity - The software becomes too hard to implement changes. Each change causes cascading changes.
    • Fragility - When a part of the software is changed, another part breaks which has no relation.
    • Immobility - When it's too hard to reuse part of software leading to rewriting it again.
    • Viscosity(design) - When multiple ways are present to implement change, the design preserving change being the hard one to implement.
    • Viscosity(environment) - The implementation of change is slow and inefficient.

    SOLID principles are some of the principles that will help design better software from start.

    Single Responsibility Principle3

    The Single Responsibility Principle states that each software module should have one and only one reason to change. The 'only one reason to change' means that whenever there is a change request from a person/department/team on software when done should result in a new version of the software with a change in only one particular part of the software. No change request from another person/department/team should result in changes in the same part of the software.

    It essentially means that software should be separated into different classes or modules depending upon from which stakeholder the request arises, therefore meaning that the class or module has a single responsibility.

    Example:

    class Employee(): def calculate_pay(): pass def save(): pass def report_hours(): pass

    For this example, consider an organization with CFO, CTO, and COO at the top. Here the calculate_pay method is allocated to CFO, the report_hours method is allocated to the COO, save method which saves all data would be allocated to CTO inside the Employee class. Whenever there is any change by any C-level person, it would not affect other parts of the employee which are allocated to other C-level persons.

    Open-Closed Principle

    The Open-Closed Principle states that a module should be open for extension but closed for modification. It means that when we want to change the modules, we should be able to change them without changing the code of the modules. This is achieved through the use of abstraction and polymorphism.

    Example:

    class Modem(ABC): @abstractmethod def dial(self, phoneNumber): pass class AnalogModem(Modem): def dial(self, phoneNumber): print(f"Dialing {phoneNumber} using an analog modem.") class DigitalModem(Modem): def dial(self, phoneNumber): print(f"Dialing {phoneNumber} using a digital modem.") def logOn(modem: Modem, phoneNumber, username, password): modem.dial(phoneNumber) analog_modem = AnalogModem()
digital_modem = DigitalModem() logOn(analog_modem, "12345", "user", "pass")
logOn(digital_modem, "67890", "user", "pass")

    Here in the above example, the logOn function is open for modification by implementing analog and digital modems but, is closed from the logOn function. When a new type of modem is created in the future there is no need to modify logOn code but the logOn can be changed.

    Liskov Substitution Principle

    The Liskov Substitution Principle states that subclasses should be suitable for their base classes. When using inheritance the derived class must be able to substitute the base class in usage. If there is any significant change between the base class and the derived class then there shouldn't be inheritance.

    Example:

    class Animal: def make_sound(self): pass class Dog(Animal): def make_sound(self): print("bark") class Cat(Animal): def make_sound(self): print("meow") def make_animal_sound(animal: Animal): animal.make_sound() # Usage:
dog = Dog()
cat = Cat() make_animal_sound(dog) # bark
make_animal_sound(cat) # meow

    In the above example, we can pass both cat and dog instances with the Animal base class as an argument to the function and it still works as expected.

    Interface Segregation Principle

    The Interface Segregation Principle states that many client interfaces are better than one general-purpose interface. It means that if a class has several clients, rather than loading the class with all the methods that the clients need, having specific interfaces for each client and multiplying inherit them into class. This will help the client to inherit a class where they don't need to know the parts of the interface that they won't use.

    Example:

    class Printer(ABC): @abstractmethod def print(self, document): pass class Fax(ABC): @abstractmethod def fax(self, document): pass class Scanner(ABC): @abstractmethod def scan(self, document): pass class MultiFunctionDevice(Printer, Fax, Scanner): def print(self, document): print(f"Printer") def fax(self, document): print(f"Fax") def scan(self, document): print(f"Scanner") class SimplePrinter(Printer): def print(self, document): print(f"modern printer")

    Here the interfaces Printer, Fax, and Scanner are implemented in all or separately instead of one big fat PrintFaxScan class even though they are closely related in the real world. The person implementing only one interface does not know the others.

    Dependency Inversion Principle

    The Interface Segregation Principle states "Depend upon Abstractions. Do not depend upon concretions.". When a class depends upon other classes, it can result in repeated code. Every dependency in design should target an interface or abstract class. No dependency should target the concrete class. This is to ensure that a change in one part of the code won't result in a break in another of the code.

    Example:

    class Driver: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def drive(self, vehicle): vehicle.drive() class Vehicle: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def drive(self): pass class Car(Vehicle): def drive(self): print(f"Driving {self.name}") class Boat(Vehicle): def drive(self): print(f"Sailing {self.name}")

    In the above example, if the Driver is created by extending Car and Boat, it'll result in repeated code and will require cascading changes to implement change to Driver. Having the Driver target the class Vehicle will help it to automatically use the Car and Boat class.
item.author: __saravanan__

item.title: 使用 Gitpod 在浏览器中尝试 askui | Try askui in Your Browser With Gitpod
item.description:
因此，将它安装在本地计算机上可能会很痛苦。 并非一切都可以开箱即用，或者只是缺少权限（看看你的 macOS 🫣）。

但是我们有一个解决方案，我们将在这篇博文中向您展示！

我们的 GitHub 存储库中有一个随时可用的 Gitpod 配置，您可以在浏览器中免费试用 askui，没有任何安装问题。

您只需要一个 GitHub 帐户和一个 Gitpod 帐户 🦄

设置

前往Gitpod.io ，使用您的 Github 帐户创建一个帐户，然后就可以开始了！

我们的存储库还具有已配置的访问令牌和工作区 ID，因此您只需单击“在 Gitpod 中打开”按钮即可开始。

启动 Gitpod 工作区

访问我们准备好的 Git-Repository并单击按钮Open in Gitpod 。 这应该会将您重定向到 Gitpod 并自动打开 Gitpod 中的 Git-Repository。

注意：启动可能需要几分钟，因为这是自定义构建！

如果您在必须选择代码编辑器之前从未在 Gitpod 中打开过存储库。 选择默认的，应该是 Visual Studio Code。

整理你的 Gitpod 工作区

首先，我们将整理您的工作区，因为浏览器中的 Visual Studio Code 编辑器杂乱无章：

  • 隐藏边栏：按CTRL + b （在 macOS 上为COMMAND + b ）
  • 删除入门选项卡：左键单击选项卡下方的x

之后您的浏览器窗口应如下所示：

打开工作区的 GUI

在这个工作区启动时，askui 会在后台安装，并且第一个自动化已经开始！

单击选项卡Simple Browser查看此工作区的 GUI（Linux 桌面）。 您看到的是来自物理设备的真实 GUI，但在云端。 如果您在元素周围看到红色框，如下图所示，这意味着自动化已准备好运行：

您还可以调整选项卡的大小以获得更好的查看体验。

或者，您可以通过打开“端口”选项卡并单击运行 Gitpod 环境的虚拟桌面的端口6080 的“打开预览”图标，在不同的选项卡中打开它以获得最大视图大小：

观察第一次 askui 自动化运行

单击Simple Browser选项卡以将焦点置于 GUI 中。

您应该会看到文本编辑器 (Geany) 已打开，并且其中已包含一些文本。 在进入下一段之前阅读它。

然后按键盘上的ESC 键，观察 askui 键入更多文本，移动鼠标，打开 Google Chrome，然后通过键入 URL 并按键盘上的 ENTER 键导航到网页。 在这两者之间应该有一些弹出窗口可以点击 😉

为了更好地理解：

具有真实的鼠标移动、击键和元素的视觉选择。

如果您想知道代码如何实现这一点，请单击选项卡my-first-askui-test-suite.test.ts以查看带有每条指令注释的代码。

现在去哪里

您现在已经了解了远程云环境中的简单自动化可以做什么。

前往我们的用户门户网站，在您的本地机器上免费注册（和使用）askui。

对于本地安装，请遵循Onboarding Guide 。

如果您需要更多灵感，我们为您提供以下资源：

  • 我们的Discord 社区可以回答任何问题。

    奖金

    如果你想在 Gitpod 中看到 askui 的实际效果，还有一个视频：



原文:

askui controls your operating system (OS) through mouse movement, mouseclicks, keypresses and screencapture.

Thus it can be a pain to install it on your local machine. Not everything might work out of the box or there are simply permissions missing (looking at you macOS 🫣).

But we have a solution we will show you in this blog post!

Our GitHub repository has a ready-to-go Gitpod configuration inside where you can try out askui for free in your browser without any installation issues.

All you need is a GitHub account and a Gitpod account 🦄

Setup

Head over to Gitpod.io, create an account with your Github account, and you're ready to go!

Our repository also has an access token and workspace id already configured so you can just click the Open in Gitpod button and get started.

Start the Gitpod Workspace

Visit our prepared Git-Repository and click on the Button Open in Gitpod. This should redirect you to Gitpod and automatically open the Git-Repository in Gitpod.

Note: Startup may take a few minutes as this is a custom build!

If you never opened a repository in Gitpod before you have to select a code editor. Choose the default one, which should be Visual Studio Code.

Tidy Up your Gitpod Workspace

First, we will tidy up your workspace as the Visual Studio Code Editor in your browser is cluttered:

  • Hide Sidebar: press CTRL + b (COMMAND + b on macOS)
  • Remove Get Started tab: Left click on the x beneath the tab

Your browser window should look like this afterward:

Open the GUI of the workspace

On startup of this workspace, askui gets installed in the background and the first automation already started!

Click on the tab Simple Browser to see this workspace's GUI (Linux Desktop). What you see is a real GUI from a physical device, but in the cloud. If you see red boxes, like the one in the next picture, around elements, it means the automation is ready to run:

You can also resize the tab to have a better viewing experience.

Or you could open it in a different tab for maximum view size by opening the Ports tab and click on Open Preview icon for port 6080 where the virtual desktop of the Gitpod environment is running:

Observe First askui Automation Run

Click in the Simple Browser tab to get the focus into the GUI.

You should see that a text editor (Geany) has been opened and some text is already in there. Read it before you go to the next paragraph.

Then press ESC on your keyboard and observe that askui types in some more text, moves the mouse, opens Google Chrome, and navigates to a webpage by typing in the URL and pressing ENTER on your keyboard. In between there should be some popups that get clicked away 😉

For better understanding:

askui automates your Operating System as a real human would! With real mouse movement, keystrokes, and visual selection of elements.

If you want to know how the code looks to achieve this, click on the tab my-first-askui-test-suite.test.ts to see the code with comments for each instruction.

Where To Go Now

You have now seen what simple automation in a remote cloud environment can do.

Head over to our User Portal to register (and use) askui for free on your local machine.

For local installation follow the Onboarding Guide.

If you need more inspiration we have the following resources for you:

  • askui docs
  • Flutter Example Repository
  • Example askui Automations on Github

And our Discord-Community to answer any questions.

Bonus

There is also a video if you want to see askui in Gitpod in action:
item.author: johannesdienst

item.title: 在菲律宾杜马格特开设新办事处，为 STEM、学术和贸易出版商提供高价值的编辑服务 | New Office Opened in Dumaguete, Philippines to Offer High-Value Editorial Services for STEM, Academic, and Trade Publishers
item.description:
发表于 2 月 20 日

该站点旨在为客户提供一流的 STEM、学术和贸易书籍以及期刊出版商编辑服务。

该公司已经存在超过 30 年，为全球出版商提供编辑服务、项目管理、制作和技术开发。 这个新办公室将使 Nova Techset 能够利用杜马格特地区的宝贵专业知识，该地区传统上与出版编辑和制作技能相关，为我们的客户提供一流的产品和编辑服务。 之所以选择杜马格特，也是因为它是一座大学城，当地居民的教育水平高，英语读写能力出色。 新地点靠近 Silliman 大学，我们的目标是在该大学与 Nova Techset 之间建立密切合作。

我们希望扩大我们在杜马格特的足迹，为我们的客户提供高价值的服务。”

一旦暂停，novatechset2 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，novatechset2 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，novatechset2 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 novatechset2 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 novatechset2：

降低 novatechset2 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 novatechset2 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

novatechset2

Posted on Feb 20

Nova Techset is proud to announce the opening of its new office in Dumaguete, Philippines, offering high-value editorial services. The site is designed to provide customers with best-in-class editorial services for STEM, Academic & Trade Book, and journal Publishers. 

On January 20, 2023, the Vice Mayor, Department of Trade and Industry Director, Information and Communications Technology Association (ICT) heads, university & college deans and professors, government officials, and heads of national offices witnessed the inauguration of the newly created branch office of Nova Techset Limited in Dumaguete City.

Nova Techset Ltd is a technology-driven service provider offering a full range of editorial and production services to the global publishing community. The company has been in existence for more than three decades delivering Editorial Services, Project Management, Production, and Technology Development for publishers across the globe. Nova Techset has a reputation of providing high-quality service with timely delivery and has developed a robust module-based technology platform to integrate and streamline the editorial and production processes involved in the publishing of STEM, Academic and Trade books and journals.

This new office will enable Nova Techset to utilize the valuable expertise to be found in the Dumaguete area, an area traditionally associated with publishing editorial and production skills, to offer our customers best in class products and editorial services. Dumaguete has also been chosen because it is a university city and the local population has a high level of education and excellent written and spoken English skills. The new site is close to Silliman University and our aim is to have close collaboration between the university and Nova Techset. 

“We are thrilled to launch this new site and offer our customers a better way to experience Nova Techset,” said Yogesh Agarwal MD & CEO of Nova Techset. “We immediately identified with the people in Dumaguete, not only through their language and subject matter skills but also the family values and great attitude that we have in our India offices. We want to grow our footprint in Dumaguete to supply high value services to our clients."

Nova Techset plans to staff the new office with project managers, copyeditors, peer review management and author support services staff, and software developers, during the first year.

Michael Andreuzza - Feb 19

Once suspended, novatechset2 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, novatechset2 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by novatechset2 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If novatechset2 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag novatechset2:

Make all posts by novatechset2 less visible

novatechset2 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging novatechset2 will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: novatechset2

item.title: 为什么介绍视频对 YouTube 频道如此重要？ | Why are introduction videos so important for YouTube channels?
item.description:
拉惹加格

发表于 2 月 20 日

多年来，YouTube 已成为最常用的应用程序之一。 内容创作者非常出色地利用了该平台。 平台每天生产和发布数百万内容。 通过YouTube频道，不仅可以赚钱，还可以赚取商誉（当然前提是你的内容不错，被观众欣赏）。 或者你可以说，通过在线创作内容获得的名声是 YouTube 的加分点。 有那么多白手起家的人，现在已经家喻户晓了。 我真的很想谈谈它。 他们是 PayPal 的前雇员。 除此之外，YouTube 上的第一个视频于 2005 年 4 月 23 日上传。但是您知道 YouTube 最引人注目的事实是什么吗？ 它最初是作为约会应用程序推出的。 随机的人可以在这里谈论他们对视频的个人兴趣，然后将其上传到 YouTube。 这可能是它在 2005 年 2 月 14 日（即情人节）推出的原因。 但由于很多人都不敢上传这样的视频，联合创始人放弃了约会应用的想法，让它继续作为一个免费的视频发布平台。 慢慢地，人们有了一个惊人的想法，通过展示他们的才华和技能来利用这个平台。 如果你还记得的话，你就会知道很多当红明星的突破时刻都是从 YouTube 开始的。 如果您打算开设自己的 YouTube 频道？ 但是，如果您对编辑一窍不通，那么您绝对应该通过 InVideo Shopping 网站的“学习”部分来学习它。 您还可以使用InVideo 促销代码以极低的价格购买他们的编辑计划。

当然，他们的旅程也不会一帆风顺。 他们也不得不面对和经历挣扎，但毕竟现在是值得的。 因为他们在各自的行业和领域拥有良好的声誉和声誉。 但是你有没有观察到，随着时间的流逝，上传到 YouTube 上的内容也经历了一些节制。 每天都有新趋势引起每个人的注意并鼓励人们开始发布内容。 新推出的“YouTube Shorts”也成为了关注的焦点。 那些15-60秒的视频被有才华的人雕刻了这么多内容。 现在的人更喜欢短片而不是冗长的视频。 比如，长视频短片也可以在任何地方制作。 没有这种强迫症。 但是，如果我们主要谈论那些自 YouTube 开始其旅程以来我们一直在制作的冗长的旧视频。 这些视频也经历了很多节制和改造。 一个主要的显着变化是介绍视频，让我们进一步了解它。 如果您不知道如何创建自己的介绍视频，请在 inVideo 网站上查看令人惊叹的即用型模板，并索取您的 inVideo 折扣代码。 他们的保费计划也可以打折购买，您甚至可以通过 inVideo 优惠券代码以更优惠的价格获得。

什么是 YouTube 频道上的介绍视频？

介绍视频是您提供有关您自己的信息的视频。 作为信息，如果您是出于商业目的，您可以输入您的公司名称。 即使您拥有自己的个人频道，您也可以添加您的姓名、徽标以及与您的内容相关的 1-2 行内容。 除此之外，在某些情况下，您还可以添加视频的标题。 为什么介绍视频对 YouTube 频道如此重要？

因为随着时代的变迁，YouTube 视频已经成为商业机构推广产品和服务的一个很好的工具。 告诉他们，即使是您名下的企业也存在。 因为直到人们不知道您的公司名称是什么？ 你来自哪个位置，他将如何成为你的客户？ 免费试用后，您可以使用 inVideo 优惠券购买高级计划。 优惠券将使您在账单金额中获得额外的扣除。

此外，我们不仅需要制作视频并上传并展示给人们看，还需要邀请人们作为客户加入我们。 所以为此，观众有必要掌握一些关于我们的特殊信息，这样他才能认出我们。 当您在视频中添加介绍片段时，它会帮助观众进一步观看您的视频。 自己想一想，在观看了与您的任何产品相关的视频后，您喜欢该产品并且现在想购买它。 但是视频中既没有告诉你那个品牌的名字，也没有告诉你如何购买。 所以你会在中间沮丧地停止视频，对吧？

不想再看那个品牌的频道视频？ 这就是介绍视频很重要的原因。 您还可以为您的观众提供超值优惠，以建立庞大的客户群。 如果您不知道如何创建令人兴奋的交易，请查看 inVideo 交易。 这些交易超级有吸引力。

但是是的，在某些情况下不需要介绍视频，例如 youtube 短片。 因为短片只有 15-60 秒长，您将如何添加介绍视频？ 所以在这种情况下，避免介绍视频。 但是当制作2分钟或2分钟以上的长度视频时，则在其中包含一个小的介绍部分。 您不需要在视频中添加那么多花哨的元素和视觉效果来使其看起来很吸引人，但只需添加一些简单的内容即可达到最佳效果。 由于视频优惠非常吸引人，您应该看看它们。

原文:

Rajat Garg

Posted on Feb 20

YouTube has become one of the most used apps over the years. The utilization of the platform is very fabulously done by content creators. Million of content is produced and published on the platform daily. And the fact that YouTube pays is the most attractive part of being a youtuber. Through the YouTube channel, not only can you earn money but also can earn goodwill ( of course if your content is good and appreciated by the audience). Or you can say that the name and fame gained through creating content online is the bonus point of YouTube. There are so many people who started from scratch but now they are known by everyone. YouTube also has its own success story. And I would really like to talk about it. YouTube was first founded by Jawed Karim, Chad Hurley and Steve Chen. They were ex employees at PayPal. Apart from this the first video on YouTube was uploaded on 23rd April 2005. But you know what the most eye-catching fact about YouTube? It was initially launched as a dating app. Where random people can talk about their personal interest in a video and then upload it on YouTube. And this might be the reason it was launched on 14th February 2005 i.e the Valentine's day. But as many people were too shy to upload such videos, the co-founders dropped the dating app idea and let it continue as a free video publishing platform. Slowly and steadily people got an amazing idea to utilize the platform by showcasing their talent and skills. If you can recall, you will get to know that so many popular celebrities' breakthrough moments had begun from YouTube. If you are planning to start your own YouTube channel? But if you have no idea about editing, then you should definitely learn it through InVideo Shopping website's "Learn" Section. Also you can purchase their editing plan at a very minimal price using InVideo Promo Codes.

Of Course their journey wouldn't be easy as well. They also had to face and go through struggle but after all it is worth it now. As they are holding a good fame and name in their respective industry and field. But have you observed that as the time flew, the content uploaded on YouTube has also gone through some moderation. Everyday a new trend catches everyone's attention and encourages people to start posting something. Also the new "YouTube Shorts" has become the center of attention. Those 15-60 seconds videos are engraved with so much content by talented people. People nowadays love shorts more than lengthy videos. Like, Lengthy videos shorts can also be created in any area. There's no such compulsion. But if we talk mainly about those old lengthy videos, which we have been creating ever since YouTube began its journey. The videos have also gone through a lot of moderation and transformation. And one main noticeable change is the Introduction video, so let's know about it more. If you don't know how to create your own introduction video, checkout the amazing ready to use templates at inVideo website and claim your inVideo Discount Codes. Also their premium plan is available at discount and you can get it even at a more discounted price through inVideo Coupon Codes.

What is an Introduction video on YouTube channel?

Introduction video is a video in which you give information about yourself. As information you can enter your business name if you are doing business purpose. And even if you have your own personal channel, you can add your name, your logo, and 1-2 lines related to your content. Along with this, you can also add the title of the video in some cases. A free signup version is available at inVideo.

Why are Introduction videos so important for YouTube channels?

Because with the changing times, YouTube videos have become a very good tool for business organizations to promote their products and services. Aise me Introduction videos ek bahut acha tarika hai new audience tak apni janakari pahuchane ka. Tell them that even a business in your name exists. Because until people will not know what is the name of your business? From which location are you, how will he become your customer? After the free trial you can purchase the premium plan using inVideo Coupons. The Coupons will let you get additional deduction in the billing amount.

Also, we do not only have to make videos and upload them and show them to people, but also invite people to our side as a customer. So for this, it is necessary for the viewer to have some special information about us, so that he can recognize us. YouTube will let you get live only after the prescribed subscription on your channel, but the inVideo Sale is live and will let you get any editing services at much lower cost. When you add an introduction segment to your video, it will help the viewers to watch your video further. Think for yourself that after watching a video related to any of your products, you liked that product and now you want to buy it. But neither the name of that brand was told to you in the video nor how to buy it. So you will stop the video in the middle in frustration, right?

And don't want to see further channel videos of that brand? This is the reason why introduction videos are important. You can also offer amazing deals to your viewers to create a great customer base. And if you don't know how to create exciting deals, please checkout the inVideo Deals. The deals are super attractive.

But yes introduction videos are not required in some cases like youtube shorts. Because shorts are only 15-60 seconds long, how will you add an introduction video? So In such cases, avoid introduction videos. But when making a length video which is of 2 or more than 2 minutes, then include a small introductory part in it. You don't need to add so many fancy elements and visuals to your video in order to make it look appealing but just some simple additions will work out best. As inVideo Offers are super appealing, you should have a look at them.

Helitha Rupasinghe - Jan 28

item.title: 可靠的机场出租车服务的重要性 | The Importance of Reliable Airport Taxi Services
item.description:
可靠的机场出租车服务对于需要准时往返机场的旅客来说至关重要。 无论您是出差还是度假，可靠的机场出租车服务都可以让您的旅行取得成功。 可靠的机场出租车服务的最大好处之一是让您高枕无忧。 知道您会在需要时乘车前往机场，这消除了试图寻找最后一分钟乘车或处理前往机场途中交通的压力。 此外，许多机场出租车服务都提供在线预订和移动应用程序，让您可以轻松提前安排行程，因此您知道在您需要的时候可以乘车。 可靠的机场出租车服务对于确保您准时到达机场也很重要。 良好的机场出租车服务会监控交通并相应调整路线，以确保您有充足的时间到达机场办理登机手续和通过安检。 这对于时间紧迫且不能错过航班的商务旅客尤其重要。 可靠的机场出租车服务的另一个好处是他们能够处理您的行李。 信誉良好的机场出租车服务将拥有足够宽敞的车辆来容纳您的行李，并且司机愿意帮助您装卸行李。 这对于携带大量行李或易碎物品需要格外小心的旅客来说是一种极大的安慰。 使用机场出租车服务有很多重要性 首先，这是一种安全可靠的往返机场的方式。 机场出租车服务是经过许可和投保的，因此您可以放心在旅途中是安全的。 其次，机场出租车服务是一种便捷的出行方式。 你可以提前预订出租车，他们会到你家或办公室接你，然后直接送你去机场。 无需担心在机场寻找停车位或处理交通问题。 第三，机场出租车服务舒适。 您可以在出租车后座放松身心，享受旅程。 司机很专业，会准时送你到目的地。 总之，可靠的机场出租车服务对于需要准时往返机场的旅客来说是必不可少的。 他们提供安心、准时和安全，这些都是确保成功旅行的重要因素。 在可靠的机场出租车服务的帮助下，旅客可以高枕无忧，因为他们知道他们会准时到达机场并且行李安全地被拖走。

原文:

Reliable airport taxi services are essential for travelers who need to get to and from the airport on time. Whether you're going on a business trip or a vacation, a reliable airport taxi service can make all the difference in the success of your trip. One of the biggest benefits of a reliable airport taxi service is the peace of mind it provides. Knowing that you'll have a ride to the airport when you need it eliminates the stress of trying to find a last-minute ride or dealing with traffic on the way to the airport. Plus, many airport taxi services offer online booking and mobile apps, making it easy to schedule a ride in advance, so you know you'll have a ride when you need it. Reliable airport taxi services are also important for making sure you arrive at the airport on time. A good airport taxi service will monitor traffic and adjust the route accordingly to ensure that you arrive at the airport with plenty of time to check in and go through security. This is especially important for business travelers who have tight schedules and can't afford to miss their flights. Another benefit of a reliable airport taxi service is their ability to handle your luggage. Reputable airport taxi services will have vehicles that are spacious enough to accommodate your luggage, and drivers who are willing to help you load and unload your bags. This is a great relief for travelers who have a lot of luggage or fragile items that need extra care. There are many Importance of using an airport taxi service First, it is a safe and reliable way to get to and from the airport. Airport taxi services are licensed and insured, so you can be sure that you will be safe during your travels. Second, airport taxi services are a convenient way to travel. You can book your taxi in advance, and they will pick you up at your home or office and take you directly to the airport. There is no need to worry about finding parking at the airport or dealing with traffic. Third, airport taxi services are comfortable. You can relax in the backseat of the taxi and enjoy the ride. The drivers are professional and will get you to your destination on time. In conclusion, reliable airport taxi services are essential for travelers who need to get to and from the airport on time. They provide peace of mind, punctuality, and safety, which are all important factors to ensure a successful trip. With the help of reliable airport taxi services, travelers can sit back and relax, knowing that they'll get to the airport on time and with their luggage safely in tow.

Airport Taxi Auckland
